parts.description: Turret AI: when a target tag is within range, fire a projectile at it on a cooldown. The aiming counterpart to the player-driven shoot. Optional priorityKey/priorityOrder targeting — prefer the most-advanced creep (follow-path __pathProgress) or the strongest/weakest by an entity-state key — defaults to nearest.

targetTag.description: Tag of entities to fire at.

priorityKey.description: Entity-state key to rank in-range targets by (e.g. '__pathProgress' for first/most-advanced, 'hp' for strongest); unset = nearest.

parts.description: Pursue the nearest entity of a target tag in full 2D, with optional axis lock and acceleration. Generalizes the SDK follow-entity-axis primitive. One of the four reuse-proof parts.

targetTag.description: Tag of the entity to pursue.

stopDistance.description: Center distance within which it holds still.

accel.description: Optional approach acceleration in px/sec² (0 = snap).

parts.description: Flee from the nearest entity of a threat tag — the mirror of ai-chase — panicking only within an optional radius. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

threatTag.description: Tag of the entity to flee from.

panicDistance.description: Only flee when the threat is within this distance (0 = always).

parts.description: Patrol between waypoints with dwell time at each, ping-pong or loop — the guard/sentry pattern. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

points.description: Array of { x, y } patrol waypoints (structural).

waitTime.description: Dwell time at each waypoint in seconds.

pingPong.description: Reverse at the ends instead of looping.

arriveRadius.description: Distance at which a waypoint is reached (structural).

parts.description: Drift in a heading that re-randomizes periodically, turning away from world edges; deterministic via world.rng. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

edgePadding.description: Distance from a wall at which it turns inward (structural).

parts.description: Force a constant velocity each tick, optionally wrapping back to the opposite edge — scrolling worlds/tiles or auto-advancing scroller obstacles. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

vx.description: Constant horizontal scroll velocity in px/sec.

vy.description: Constant vertical scroll velocity in px/sec.

wrap.description: Re-enter from the opposite edge on exit.

parts.description: A pickup consumed when a collector tag touches it, crediting a score and/or grant key and emitting a collect event. Coins, gems, power-ups, keys.

collectorTag.description: Tag of the entity that can collect.

scoreKey.description: Optional world.state score key to credit.

grantKey.description: Optional second world.state key to credit.

kind.description: Item label included in the event.

parts.description: Deal damage to overlapping entities of a target tag, with per-target cooldown, optional knockback and self-destruct. One of the four reuse-proof parts.

cooldown.description: Seconds before re-hitting the same target (0 = every tick).

knockback.description: Impulse applied to the victim away from this entity.

selfDestruct.description: Destroy this entity after a successful hit.

sound.description: Sound key on a successful hit.

parts.description: Orient an entity by writing entity.rotation (radians) each tick — the data path to a directional sprite, since the renderer honors rotation. Modes: velocity (point along travel), target (track the nearest tagged entity), pointer (twin-stick aim at the cursor), tilt (bank by a velocity axis, e.g. a flyer pitching with vertical speed). Visual only; collision stays axis-aligned.

offset.description: Radians added to the computed angle, to correct sprite art that doesn't point +x.

minSpeed.description: In 'velocity' mode, hold facing below this speed (px/s).

tiltPerVel.description: In 'tilt' mode, radians of bank per px/s of the chosen axis.

maxTilt.description: In 'tilt' mode, clamp magnitude in radians (0 = unclamped).

parts.description: The side-scroller flip convention: sets the sign of entity.scaleX from horizontal velocity so a side-view sprite faces the way it moves (moving right = scaleX positive, left = negative). The renderer honors a negative scaleX as a horizontal flip, so this just wires that to motion. Preserves the scale magnitude and holds facing below a threshold (a stopped sprite keeps its last facing). Distinct from face-angle, which rotates; this only mirrors left/right. Visual only. Order it after the mover.

invert.description: Set true when the art faces LEFT by default (default false = faces right).

parts.description: Travel an ordered list of waypoints, advancing on arrival; loops or stops at the end. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator. Records a monotonic __pathProgress (cumulative distance) so other parts can rank movers by how far along the path they are (e.g. ai-aim-and-fire 'first' targeting).

points.description: Array of { x, y } waypoints (structural geometry).

arriveRadius.description: Distance at which a waypoint is reached (structural).

loop.description: Restart from the first point at the end.

parts.description: Own an entity's hit points and death: seed hp, count an optional lifespan down, then emit a death event, tally a score key, and destroy. One of the four reuse-proof parts.

hp.description: Starting hit points (only if state.hp unset).

hpStateKey.description: Optional world.state key whose number, when set, seeds hp instead of the static hp param (cross-context carry-over).

lifespan.description: Seconds to live before dying of old age (0 = no limit).

tallyKey.description: Optional world.state key incremented on death.

parts.description: On a cooldown'd key press, spawn a short-lived hitbox entity in the facing direction. Damage and despawn reuse contact-damage + health-and-death lifespan.

hitbox.description: Entity-definition for the transient strike volume.

attackKeys.description: Key codes that trigger a swing.

reach.description: { x, y } hitbox offset from center (structural).

faceVelocity.description: Place the hitbox in the movement direction.

parts.description: Four-directional keyboard movement (arrows + WASD). Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

normalizeDiagonal.description: Scale diagonal speed to match cardinals.

parts.description: Discrete grid-stepping movement on a fixed cadence; continuous (Snake) or step-per-press (Sokoban). Writes position directly — no velocity integrator needed. An optional `moveAction` reads a logical input action (input-actions) so keyboard and a touch d-pad steer through one channel — no synthesized key events.

snap.description: Snap to the tile grid each step.

moveAction.description: Optional logical-action name to steer by instead of raw keys — unifies keyboard + touch via input-actions. Unset = key path (default, byte-identical).

parts.description: Side-scrolling locomotion: horizontal run, gravity, jump, and coyote-time. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator. Treats entity.body.contacts.onGround (set by the collision-resolution phase when a collider rests on a solid) as grounded, so a tile floor or solid body satisfies the jump test. The genre-feel layer is OPTIONAL params, each defaulting to the original behavior: run acceleration/friction (accel/friction), variable jump height (jumpCutMultiplier), jump buffering (jumpBuffer), apex hang (apexGravityMult/apexThreshold), and drop-through (down + jump on a one-way platform falls through it, via down/dropThroughTime). Optional LADDER climb: with climbSpeed set, when the entity's center is over a ladderProp tile and up/down is held it climbs (gravity off, vy = ±climbSpeed) and can step off the side.

jumpSpeed.description: Upward jump launch velocity in px/sec.

maxFall.description: Terminal fall speed in px/sec (0 = uncapped).

accel.description: Horizontal acceleration in px/sec² toward the target speed (0 = instant vx).

friction.description: Horizontal deceleration in px/sec² when there is no input (0 = use accel; only applies when accel > 0).

jumpCutMultiplier.description: vy is multiplied by this on jump-release while rising (1 = no cut / fixed-impulse jump).

jumpBuffer.description: Seconds a jump press is remembered so it fires on landing (0 = off).

apexGravityMult.description: Gravity multiplier while |vy| < apexThreshold (1 = no change).

apexThreshold.description: |vy| in px/sec under which apexGravityMult applies (0 = off).

down.description: Drop-through modifier key codes (empty = drop-through off).

dropThroughTime.description: Seconds the drop-through window stays open after down+jump on a one-way platform (0 = off).

ladderProp.description: Tile-property flag marking a climbable ladder cell (default "ladder").

climbSpeed.description: Ladder climb speed in px/sec (0 = ladders off).

up.description: Ladder climb-up key codes (default ArrowUp/W; on a ladder these climb, not jump).

parts.description: Top-down movement with normalized 8-direction keys and optional move-toward-pointer steering. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator.

pointerFollow.description: Steer toward the active pointer when no key is held.

parts.description: Teleport entities of a tag to a fixed point or a paired exit entity on a fresh contact edge. Timer-free: fires once on arrival, again only after the entrant leaves and returns; an entrant placed onto a portal by its pair is never bounced back.

tag.description: Tag of entities that can use the portal.

to.description: { x, y } destination (structural; used when targetId unset).

targetId.description: Id of an entity whose position is the destination.

parts.description: Ramp a live entity field (velocity or an entity-state value like hp) by a factor 1 + perLevel*(level-1) read from a world.state level counter. set/multiply/once modes cover auto-scroll speed ramps and per-enemy speed/hp scaling for endless/horde games.

levelKey.description: world.state key holding the 1-based difficulty level (default 'level').

perLevel.description: Fractional increase of the base per level above 1.

mode.description: 'set' (force base*factor each tick) | 'multiply' (rescale live value) | 'once' (one-time, e.g. hp).

base.description: Level-1 value for set/once on a single axis or state key.

baseX.description: Level-1 vx for set with target 'velocity'.

baseY.description: Level-1 vy for set with target 'velocity'.

parts.description: Fire projectiles on a cooldown — on key press or automatically — in a fixed direction or aimed at a tag. Spawns a clone of the projectile definition.

projectile.description: Entity-definition to spawn as the projectile.

fireKeys.description: Key codes that trigger a shot.

direction.description: { x, y } launch vector when not aiming (structural).

aimTag.description: Aim at nearest entity with this tag.

parts.description: Data-driven platformer animation: maps an entity's motion state (grounded via __onGround, horizontal speed, vertical direction) to a named sheet animation each tick and advances its frames, so a sprite switches idle -> run -> jump -> fall -> land with no hand-wired play param. The land clip is a non-looping one-shot fired on touchdown that holds until it finishes, then returns to idle/run. Each clip name is a param (default to its conventional name; set one to empty to disable that state). No-op for non-sheet sprites; pair with face-velocity for left/right flip. Order it after the collision resolvers.

jump.description: Animation name for airborne + rising (default 'jump'; '' to disable).

fall.description: Animation name for airborne + falling (default 'fall'; '' to disable).

land.description: One-shot animation on touchdown (default 'land'; '' to disable).

parts.description: A volume that emits enter/exit events for a tag, can set a world.state flag while occupied, can write its own position as a respawn point (checkpoint), and can destroy entrants (hazard / kill-plane).

tag.description: Tag of entities that activate the zone.

setStateKey.description: Optional world.state boolean key, true while occupied.

setRespawnKey.description: Optional world.state key written this zone's { x, y } on entry — a checkpoint that moves the respawn point (pair with lives-respawn.respawnStateKey).

once.description: Fire only the first time, then go inert.

parts.description: Animates one numeric entity property (x, y, scaleX/scaleY/scale, rotation, opacity) from a start value to a target over a duration with an easing curve (linear, in/out/in-out-quad, out-back overshoot). Auto-starts; loop modes none (hold at target) / loop / pingpong (triangle wave). The data path to juice motions: coin pops, bobbing pickups, fade-ins, slide-ins, pulsing beacons. from defaults to the property's current value. Visual/positional only; collision uses the base AABB. Pure per-tick math (deterministic). State is namespaced per property, so independent tweens of x and opacity coexist.

from.description: Start value (whitelisted; default = the property's current value when the tween starts).

parts.description: Moves the render viewport (world.camera) to keep a tagged entity centered, with optional smoothing (ease) and a centered deadzone, clamped to the world bounds. The runtime camera for scrolling levels — pair with a scene.world larger than scene.size.

targetTag.description: Tag of the entity to keep centered (first live match).

smoothing.description: Ease factor 0..1 per tick toward the target; 1 = hard snap.

deadzone.description: Centered { w, h } box (px) the target moves freely within before the camera pans.

parts.description: Data-triggered screenshake: on a named event it starts a decaying shake, writing a transient random offset to world.camera.shakeX/shakeY each tick (the renderer adds it), so the view shakes whether or not the scene scrolls. Kept separate from the camera base, so it never disturbs camera-follow or pointer mapping. Emit the event with an optional { magnitude, duration } payload to trigger it as data; a stronger shake refreshes a weaker active one. Deterministic (offset drawn from world.rng). The engine replacement for host-side DOM canvas-translate shake.

event.description: Event name that triggers a shake (default 'shake').

magnitude.description: Default amplitude in px when the payload omits one (default 0).

duration.description: Default length in seconds when the payload omits one (default 0).

falloff.description: Amplitude decay exponent over the duration; 1 = linear, >1 = faster initial settle (default 1).

parts.description: A currency balance with optional passive income and a cap. Seeds the balance, accrues per second, and clamps. Other parts earn and spend against the key.

Derive presentation strings from live state as DATA — the replacement for a per-frame host mirror() loop. Each binding reads a world.state (or entity.state) value and floors/rounds/ceils/compacts/templates/maps it into the world.state key a text or HUD sprite binds to. No schema change (the text-sprite `bind` slot already exists). Covers: flooring a float score, compacting a currency (1.23K/4.5M), 'Wave {v}/{c}' templates, multiplying by a prestige key, reading a child entity's hp, and mapping win/lose to a label.

bindings.description: Array of { from?, fromEntity?, fallback?, mult?, format?(floor|round|ceil|compact|fixed:N), map?, mapDefault?, emptyWhenZero?, template?({v}/{c}), constant?, to } — each writes one display key. Numeric leaves must be $cfg refs.

parts.description: Install logical input ACTION bindings: map an action name to keyboard codes and/or an on-screen rect/zone, so movers read world.input.action(...)/actionVector(...) and one action is satisfiable by keyboard OR touch — no synthesized key events. Pure data; idle on desktop with no touch. Pair with a mover's `moveAction`/`thrustAction`-style param.

bindings.description: Record<actionName, { keys?: string[], rect?: {x,y,w,h}, axisKeys?: {up,down,left,right}, zone?: {x,y,radius} }> — action sources (structural/world-space coords).

parts.description: Advance a level/difficulty counter on a score threshold or when a tracked tag is fully cleared, emitting a level-up event. Manages the counter, not scene loading.

levelKey.description: world.state key holding the current level (1-based).

scoreKey.description: Score key to read in scoreGte mode.

threshold.description: Score for the first level-up in scoreGte mode.

clearTag.description: Tag whose disappearance triggers a level-up.

maxLevel.description: Stop advancing past this level, 0 = endless.

parts.description: Projects a campaign progress index (the run-store's won-set + per-level bests, loaded into world.state by `persistence`) into the FLAT per-level keys a data-authored level-select menu binds: `<id>:sel` (a boolean the gated tap-emit reads, so only CLEARED levels are pickable), `<id>:status` (cleared/locked label), `<id>:score` and `<id>:time` (best score / best time, shown for cleared levels). The scene-side companion to createRunStore + the SDK `@level:<id>` flow token; pure (no rng/clock), so determinism is untouched.

levels.description: Ordered level (scene) ids to project — typically the game's manifest.levels.

wonKey.description: world.state key of the won-set map (default 'runWon' — the run-store default).

bestKey.description: world.state key of the per-level bests map (default 'runBest').

prefix.description: Namespacing prefix for the written keys (default '' ⇒ '<id>:sel' etc.).

clearedText.description: Status label for a cleared level (default 'CLEARED').

lockedText.description: Status label for a not-yet-cleared level (default 'LOCKED').

timeTemplate.description: {v}-template for the best time in seconds, 1 dp (default '{v}s').

parts.description: Lives + respawn loop: seed lives, watch the player tag for death, spend a life and respawn a prototype after a delay (at a live checkpoint when one is set, else the static point), end the game when lives run out.

watchTag.description: Tag of the entity whose death costs a life.

respawnStateKey.description: Optional world.state key holding a live { x, y } respawn point (a checkpoint, set by trigger-zone.setRespawnKey) — overrides respawnPosition when present.

stateKey.description: world.state scratch key for this system.

parts.description: Declarative cross-run save/load. Round-trips named world.state keys through the existing storage bridge (no host JS, no wire-protocol change): restores declared keys on boot (live value wins) and writes on change or interval. Keys/slot/cadence come from manifest.persist, overridable per-instance.

keys.description: world.state keys to persist (defaults to manifest.persist.keys).

slot.description: Storage slot/namespace suffix for the save blob.

everySeconds.description: Autosave cadence in seconds (0 = save on change only).

parts.description: On a trigger event, spawn a prototype at a verified-free grid cell (centered, deterministic via world.rng, honoring the scene tilemap when present). The data replacement for hand-rolled free-cell food/pickup placement — no stacking, no out-of-bounds.

occupiedTag.description: Tag whose live entities mark a cell occupied.

excludeTags.description: Extra tags whose live entities also block their cell (e.g. a marker at a soon-to-be-occupied cell).

parts.description: Track the score and persist a high score via the SDK storage API (never raw localStorage), keeping the high-score key at the running maximum.

storageKey.description: Persistence key under the game's storage namespace.

persist.description: Write the high score to storage.

parts.description: Maintain a keyed item inventory on world.state: ensure it exists, clamp counts to capacity, and emit item-gained/item-lost events on change.

inventoryKey.description: world.state key holding the inventory record.

stateKey.description: world.state scratch key for change detection.

parts.description: Apply a world.state-driven value to a named behavior PARAM across EVERY entity carrying a tag — the shared/global counterpart to the entity-self scale-by-state. Generalizes the hand-rolled 'restamp every tower's range/cooldown on each upgrade' pattern into data. Per modifier the value is base (world.state[from], or a $cfg `base`) optionally scaled by the difficulty factor 1+perLevel*(level-1), multiplied by world.state[multKey], and clamped to [min,max], then written each tick to `param` on the tagged entities' matching behaviors. Self-healing: freshly-spawned entities and live upgrades propagate with no event wiring.

modifiers.description: Array of { tag, param, behavior?, from?, base?, levelKey?, perLevel?, multKey?, min?, max? } — each writes one behavior param across all tagged entities. Numeric leaves (base/perLevel/min/max) must be $cfg refs.

parts.description: A countdown clock that ends the game with a win or lose outcome at zero, pausable via a world.state flag. Survive-the-clock or beat-the-clock.

timeKey.description: world.state key holding the remaining time.

pauseKey.description: world.state key that pauses the timer while truthy.

parts.description: Generic afford → deduct → emit economy transaction. A part/UI sets a { id, cost } purchase request; the system spends from a currency balance and emits purchased or purchase-denied (insufficient-funds). The buy-and-place primitive currency and upgrade-tree don't cover.

currencyKey.description: world.state balance key to spend from.

requestKey.description: world.state key holding the pending { id, cost } request.

onOk.description: Event emitted on a successful purchase.

onDenied.description: Event emitted when the purchase is denied.

costs.description: Optional id → cost map for fixed-price catalogs (structural).

parts.description: A purchasable upgrade tree driven by a request flag: spend currency, bump the owned level, apply an effect to a world.state key, with cost growth and prerequisites. All costs/effects are $cfg-driven.

upgrades.description: Array of { id, cost, effectKey?, effectAmount?, maxLevel?, costGrowth?, requires? }; cost/effectAmount/maxLevel/costGrowth are $cfg values.

levelsKey.description: world.state record of upgradeId → owned levels.

requestKey.description: world.state key holding a pending buy request.

parts.description: Spawn entities in escalating waves over time from a prototype definition, with per-wave growth, max-alive cap, spawn points, and an optional level-driven density ramp (the spawn-pressure half of difficulty). One of the four reuse-proof parts.

interval.description: Seconds between individual spawns within a wave.

waveSizeGrowth.description: Added to waveSize each subsequent wave.

maxAlive.description: Cap on concurrent countTag entities, 0 = uncapped.

countTag.description: Tag counted for maxAlive and wave-clear detection.

spawnPoints.description: Array of { x, y } spawn positions, round-robin (structural).

advanceOnClear.description: Start the next wave when all countTag are gone.

stateKey.description: world.state scratch key for this spawner.

waveKey.description: world.state key exposing the current wave number.

placement.description: literal (default; spawnPoints/prototype pos) or free-cell (scatter across free grid cells).

tileSize.description: Grid cell size in px for free-cell placement (structural).

occupiedTag.description: Tag marking occupied cells for free-cell placement (default = countTag).

levelKey.description: world.state key holding the 1-based difficulty level read by the density ramp (default "level").

densityPerLevel.description: Fractional increase to wave size per level above 1 (factor 1 + perLevel·(level-1)); 0 = no ramp.

intervalPerLevel.description: Fractional shortening of the whole spawn cadence (both interval and waveDelay) per level above 1; 0 = no ramp.

parts.description: Generalized end-of-game check: a list of conditions, first match ends the game with a win or lose outcome. Conditions: a state-threshold ({ key, cmp?, value }), a live entity-count ({ tag, count?, value? } via world.query(tag).length), a state truthy/falsy flag ({ key, truthy|falsy }), and all/any composition — so a win like 'all waves complete AND zero live creeps' is data.

conditions.description: Array of conditions; each is a state-threshold { key, cmp?, value }, a flag { key, truthy|falsy }, an entity-count { tag, count?, value? } (value defaults 0), or a composite { all|any: [...] }, plus { outcome?, winner?, sound? } on top-level entries. Numeric thresholds are $cfg refs.

parts.description: A destructible block: health-and-death gives it HP and a 'block-broken' death event (bricks for breakout, crates for arenas). Damage it via a projectile's contact-damage.
A straight-flying pellet: velocity + contact-damage (self-destructs on hit) + health-and-death lifespan. Embed it as a shoot/ai-aim-and-fire projectile, or spawn directly.
A spinning coin (4-frame sheet) that adds to the score on touch (collect-on-touch). The staple collectible for every genre.
The canonical pursuing enemy: ships with ai-chase + velocity + contact-damage + health-and-death already attached. Drop it in (or feed it to wave-spawner) and it hunts the player and hurts on touch.
A guard that walks a fixed patrol route (ai-patrol) and hurts on contact, with health-and-death. Predictable hazard for platformer/maze genres; edit its points per placement.
A stationary ranged enemy: ai-aim-and-fire lobs enemy-bullets at the player within range, with health-and-death. The pressure source for arena/shmup genres.
A small, fast, low-HP swarm bug: ai-chase + contact-damage + health-and-death. Cheap to spawn in large counts via wave-spawner for survival-arena pressure.
A higher-value gem collectible (collect-on-touch). Same mechanic as coin, distinct sprite + score weight for tiered rewards.
A key collectible that increments a 'keys' tally on touch (collect-on-touch). Pair with a door/portal gated on the keys count for lock-and-key puzzles.
A piercing beam: velocity + contact-damage that does NOT self-destruct (hits everything it passes), expiring by lifespan. A heavier weapon than bullet.

A thrown bomb that flies (velocity), then detonates by lifespan emitting an 'explode' event + sound — wire the fx 'explosion' system to that event for debris. Area damage is the game's to apply on the event.
A platform that shuttles between two points (ai-patrol reused as a mover) while tagged 'ground' so players can ride it. Edit its points per placement.
A round top-down player blob (2-frame idle squash). Composes move-topdown-360 + velocity + clamp-to-world + health-and-death. The default hero for arena/survival genres.
A side-view platformer hero: move-platformer (gravity/jump/coyote-time) + velocity + health-and-death. The hero for platformer genres; expects a 'ground'-tagged world.
A keyboard-driven shooter ship: move-4dir + velocity + clamp-to-world + shoot (fires bullets upward) + health-and-death. The hero for shmup/invaders genres.
A power-up capsule collected on touch; tracks into a 'powerups' tally the game reads to apply an effect (collect-on-touch with kind 'powerup').
A passive snake body segment (the snake head's grid movement is game logic). Tagged 'snake'+'player' so it both follows the head and counts for collision. Behavior-free by design.
A spike hazard: trigger-zone emits a 'hazard' event (and can kill) when the player overlaps it. The instant-death trap for platformers/mazes.
A static solid block, tagged 'ground'+'solid' so platformer movement stands on it and collision pairs treat it as a wall. Behavior-free.
A 256×256 vertical gradient (ink → deep blue) for calm menus and abstract scenes. Stretch or tile as a scene background.
A two-layer parallax backdrop (far hills + near hills) for side-scrollers. The host scrolls each layer at its own speed factor; the near layer has a transparent sky so it overlays the far layer.
A tiling 256×256 starfield (deterministic seeded stars on ink). Set as a scene background; tile it across the world for space genres.
Camera preset: the viewport scrolls at a constant velocity (endless runners, shmups, helicopter). A host hint; pair with the auto-scroll behavior on the world. Registers no runtime type.

Camera preset: a static viewport equal to the scene size (single-screen games — arena, breakout, puzzle). A host hint; the frozen SDK renderer draws in absolute world coords, so no runtime type is registered.

A driving 132 BPM chiptune loop (walking square bass + bright triangle arpeggio) for gameplay. Generated note-by-note via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.startMusic("action")) — zero binary audio, loops seamlessly, no-ops headless.
A calm 96 BPM chiptune loop (sparse triangle pad + gentle sine melody) for title/menu screens. Generated via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.startMusic("menu")) — zero binary audio, no-ops headless.
Crisp tiny square tick for UI clicks. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("click")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Bright two-note triangle blip for pickups. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("collect")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Filtered noise burst + falling square for explosions/deaths. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("explode")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Short low square thud + noise for impacts/damage. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("hit")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Springy upward square sweep for jumps/launches. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("jump")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Falling saw+square for defeat/game-over. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("lose")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Pitched-down square zap + noise click for firing. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("shoot")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
Rising triangle arpeggio fanfare for victory. Runtime-synthesized via Web Audio (LibraryAudioPlayer.play("win")) — zero binary audio. No-ops headless.
A 5-tile dungeon tileset (16px): floor, wall, edge, accent, hazard. Procedurally generated on the fixed palette; index by name into the sheet.
A 5-tile grass/outdoor tileset (16px): ground, wall, edge, accent, hazard. Procedurally generated by gen-assets.ts on the fixed palette; index by name into the sheet.
A 5-tile neon-arcade tileset (16px): floor, wall, edge, accent, glow. Procedurally generated on the fixed palette; index by name into the sheet.
A 5-tile space/station tileset (16px): panel, hull, edge, accent, hazard. Procedurally generated on the fixed palette; index by name into the sheet.

A per-entity behavior that resizes a flat rect to value/max each tick: a health/energy/boss/progress bar. Reads the value from world.state[valueKey] by default, or — with valueEntity — from a tracked entity's own state (a data-driven health bar). Optional lowColor/lowThreshold recolor.

valueKey.description: State key holding the current value (default "hp").

valueEntity.description: Optional entity id OR tag — read the value from that entity's state[valueKey] (e.g. a player's hp) instead of world.state[valueKey]. Unset ⇒ the world.state read.

maxKey.description: Optional world.state key holding the max (overrides max when set).

width.description: Full-bar width in px (structural; the entity's authored width).

lowColor.description: Optional color applied when the ratio drops below lowThreshold.

parts.description: A health/progress bar HUD entity: a flat rect whose width the hud-bar behavior drives from world.state.hp / world.state.maxHp each tick. Anchored top-left; recolor/threshold are optional add-ons.
A score readout HUD entity: a text sprite live-bound to world.state.score (the SDK's frozen text `bind`). Behavior-free — drop it in any scene's entities and the score system updates it.
A countdown readout HUD entity: a text sprite bound to world.state.timeLeft (written by the timer-countdown system). Behavior-free.
A wave/level readout HUD entity: a text sprite bound to world.state.wave (written by wave-spawner via its waveKey). Behavior-free.

The keyboard companion to tap-emit: a per-entity behavior that emits a game event on the down-edge of any of `keys`, so title/over screens are keyboard-accessible as pure data with no host keydown bridge. Put it on the same flow-button entity as tap-emit, emitting the same event, and wire it to a scene.flow.on transition. Adopts a key held across the spawning scene change so it only fires on a fresh press.
A game-over scene fragment: 'GAME OVER', a final score readout bound to world.state.score, and a retry prompt. Load it when a lose condition fires; pairs with the sfx-lose cue.
A pause overlay scene fragment: a dimmed panel + 'PAUSED' + resume prompt. Render over the frozen gameplay scene (the host stops stepping the loop while paused).
A title-screen scene fragment: centered title + prompt text on a flat background. Load it as a scene (add a start-on-input handler in game code) before the gameplay scene; pairs with music-menu.

A per-entity behavior that emits a game event when the entity is clicked/tapped (reads the SDK click edge and the topmost entity pick). Turns a button into a pure-data scene-flow edge: wire emitOnTap to a scene.flow.on transition for title→play→over with no host code. Optional requireKey GATES the emit on a truthy world.state flag, so a button is LOCKED content (a level-select card enabled only for cleared levels, a buy button needing funds) — absent ⇒ always emits.

emitOnTap.description: Event name emitted on tap (default 'tapped'). Wire it to a scene.flow.on transition.

requireKey.description: Optional world.state key that must be truthy for the tap to emit — a data gate for locked content. Absent ⇒ always emits. A flat key (project a nested value, e.g. via level-select, first).

parts.description: On-screen mobile controls: a virtual d-pad (attach touch-dpad to your player, replacing its keyboard mover) plus a controller entity carrying two touch-button behaviors that set world.state flags ('fire', 'jump'). Includes decorative d-pad/button visuals. No-op without touch input, so desktop play is unaffected.
A per-entity BEHAVIOR that kicks up puffs only while the entity is moving above minSpeed (footsteps, landings, sliding). Emits nothing at rest.

minSpeed.description: Speed threshold below which no dust spawns.

parts.description: An event-driven particle SYSTEM: on a game event (default 'enemy-died'/'explode') it bursts fast gravity-pulled debris at the event's position. Wire a mortal entity's health-and-death.deathEvent to the same name.

event.description: Game event that triggers a burst.

colors.description: Palette hex colors cycled across particles.

parts.description: A screen fade preset for the host-side ScreenEffects controller (fade to/from a color for scene transitions and intros). Trigger fx.fadeOut(color, duration) / fx.fadeIn(...). Registers no runtime type.
A full-screen flash preset for the host-side ScreenEffects controller (a colored overlay that fades out). Trigger fx.flash(color, duration) on big hits/pickups. Registers no runtime type.

A screen-shake preset for the host-side ScreenEffects controller (the frozen renderer draws in absolute coords, so camera shake belongs to the page that owns the canvas). Trigger fx.shake(magnitude, duration) on impacts/deaths. Deterministic; registers no runtime type.

magnitude.description: Peak shake offset in px (presentational, host-applied).

parts.description: A gentle event-driven particle SYSTEM: soft upward twinkles on a game event (default 'collect') for pickups, power-ups, and level-ups. Same wiring model as explosion.

event.description: Game event that triggers a twinkle.

colors.description: Palette hex colors cycled across particles.

parts.description: A per-entity BEHAVIOR that drips a fading particle behind a moving entity at a fixed rate (projectiles, comets, dashes). Time-based, so density is frame-rate independent.
